series_description: Audits and reviews performed by or on behalf of an agency, including the working papers that support the audit. Also includes audits performed on the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description: Including any supporting documentation created and/or used to justify and support legislative appropriations requests by an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per TAC 3.34(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention
period.

series_description: Incoming/outgoing and internal leadership, supervisory, managerial, executive, administrative, substantive, and other high-level in any media, including electronic communication, pertaining to the formulation, planning, implementation, interpretation, modification, or redefinition of the programs, services, or projects of an agency and the administrative regulations, policies, and procedures that govern them.
retention_statement: 4 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact
the State Archives when these
records have met their retention
periods. CAUTION: This records
series and AIN 1.4 should be used
only for correspondence that is not
included in or directly related to
another records series on the
agency's approved schedules. For
example, a memorandum that
documents an appropriations
request must be retained for the
minimum retention period
prescribed by AIN 1.2; a letter
concerning an audit for the
prescribed by AIN 1.1, etc.

series_description: Non-Administrative incoming/outgoing and internal correspondence, in any media, pertaining to or arising from the route operations of the policies, programs, services, or projects of an agency.
retention_statement: 2 years
comments:
See comments to AIN 1.3. See also
AIN 1.5.

series_description: Any document that officially initiates, rescinds, or amends general office procedures
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description: Any document that initiates, rescinds, or amends a regulation, policy, or procedure that governs the programs, services, or projects of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description: Calendars, appointment books or programs and scheduling, or itinerary records, maintained by staff during business hours that document appointments, itineraries, and other activities of agency officials or employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state records
and subject to this retention
period. See Open Records Decision
635 issued in December 1995 by
the Attorney General. ARCHIVES
NOTES: Only the calendars,
appointment, and itinerary records
of elected officials, executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors, and program
heads require archival review.
Contact the State Archives when
these records have met their
retention periods.

series_description: From agency legal counsel or the Attorney General, including any request eliciting the opinions.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard
to pending litigation, or opinions
rendered for Public Information Act
Requests. See AIN 1.8, 1.10, and
1.11. ARCHIVES NOTE: Opinions
and advice that set legal precedent
or exhibit historical value will be
evaluated by the Archives and
Information Services Division of the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission for archival
preservation. See pages ix for
additional guidelines.

series_description: News, press releases, marketing files and collateral, or any public relations files maintained or issued by an agency. Includes print, electronic, audio, and audiovisual records. For this agency this includes Electronic Newsletters.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are furnished to the public under Public Information Act (Chapter 552
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
legal_citation: Government Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.

series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are excepted under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
legal_citation: Government Code), including records provided to the legislature undersection 552.008.

series_description: Charts or diagrams that show the structure of an agency and the relationships and relative ranks of its departments and job positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
t o the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are
not considered archival.

series_description: Plans and records relating to the process of planning new or redefined programs, services, or projects of an agency that are not included in or directly related to there records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description: Copies of all proposed, withdrawn, emergency, and adopted rules; open meeting notices; or any other documents required by law to be submitted to the Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description: Survey soliciting feedback from customers or clients of an agency, and the statistical data maintained rating an agency's performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Review by Executive Director
required before disposal. See AIN
1.27.

series_description: Notes or text of speeches, papers, presentations, or reports delivered in conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_description: Records created by or on behalf of an agency in anticipation of or in the adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation.

series_description: Operational strategic plans, which include descriptions of Information resources programs, prepared in accordance with
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.
legal_citation: Government Code, 2054.095 and 2056.002. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating the final plan.

series_description: Records of temporary usefulness that are not an integral part of a records series of an agency, that are not regularly files within an agency's recordkeeping system, and that are required only for a limited period of time for the completion of an action by an official or employee of the agency or in the preparation of an ongoing records series. Transitory records are not essential to the fulfillment of statutory obligations or to the documentation of agency functions. See page xii for examples.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title: Meeting, Agendas and Minutes of Open
series_description: Agendas and minutes of state boards, committees, commissions, and councils that conduct open meetings required by Chapter 551
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
CAUTION: This records series and
AIN 1.22, 1.23, and 1.24 must be
used for those state boards,
committees, commissions, and
councils, which by law or the
biennial Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency. ARCHIVES
NOTE: Agency of origin retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met by
sending a copy to the Archives and
Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives
Commission.

series_title: Meeting, Agenda and Minutes or Audiovisual Recordings of Closed
series_description: Agendas, minutes, or audiovisual recording of closed meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments: See caution comment in AIN 1.21.

series_description: Audiovisual recording of open meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments:
CAUTION: It is an exception to the
AC + 90 days retention that if an
agency does not prepare written
minutes, it must retain recordings
of open meetings permanently,
following the same retention
requirements of AIN 1.21. See also
caution comment on AIN 1.21.

series_description: Documents submitted at meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils, including exhibit items, documentation for agenda items, public comment forms, etc. Includes documents sent in advance of meetings for briefing purposes, some of which may not be submitted at an actual meeting.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
It is agency policy to keep the
materials used at Board meetings
longer than the TSLAC
recommendation. See caution
comment at AIN 1.21.

series_description: Any records of an agency needed for the documentation of output, outcome, efficiency, and explanatory measures in an agency's appropriations request or strategic plan, and for performance measures used to manage the agency.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
See AIN 1.28 for reports on agency
performance measures. CAUTION:
The FE + 3 retention period
overrides any shorter retention
period for a records series in this
schedule if the records series is
needed for documentation of
agency performance measures.

series_description: Biennial narrative reports to the governor and legislature as required by an agency's enabling statutes, including annual narrative reports if they are required by statute. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating final report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description: Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or studies on non-fiscal aspects of an agency's programs, services, or projects compiled by agency personnel, by advisory committees, or by consultants under contract with an agency that are not noted elsewhere in this schedules. Includes reports distributed either internally or to other entities.
retention_statement: 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Reports are
archival when they deal with
significant aspects of the agency's
programs. For agencies that have
had an archival appraisal, separate
this records series by each type of
archival coding A, R, or E. See page
ix for more information.

series_description: Quarterly and annual reports on agency performance measures submitted to executive and legislative offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
See AIN 1.25 for documentation
used to produce reports on agency
performance measures.

series_description: Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or similar records distributed internally for the use of employees or externally to the public or those individuals or entities regulated by an agency that sets out the rules, policies, and procedures that govern an agency's programs, services, or projects. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating rules, policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_title: Sunset Review Report and Related Documentation
series_description: Sunset Review Report, agency self-study, and other correspondence and supporting documentation related to the Sunset review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
self-evaluation report, agency
response to the commission's deaft
report, and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations.

series_description: Documents authorizing final disposition of records under a certified records retention schedule. Records may also include destruction authorizations (e.g. form RMD 102) approved by Texas State Library and Archives Commission
retention_statement: 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: If destruction
authorizations are maintained as
part of AIN 1.33 (Records
Disposition Logs), then longer
retention period applies.

series_description: A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR 105) that identifies the records that are created and maintained by an agency, provides the minimum timeframes the records must be retained, and includes instructions for their disposition. This series may include working files and documentation of certification and approval by the Texas State Library and Archives
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by
the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description: Logs or similar records listing records disposed by an agency, which might include records series title, dates of records, and date of disposition.
retention_statement: 10 years
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description: Worksheets or working papers used to capture records inventory information, including location tracking and to document existence and requirements of a records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description: Statute and Board Rules as published quarterly for Chapters 501, 502, 503, 505, and 507 of the Occupations Code for these agency rules.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Agency of origin retains the
permanent record copy and sends
required copies to the publications
Clearinghouse, Texas State Library.
It is agency policy to retain longer
than TSLAC minimum.

series_description: Physical and digital development and design files used to create State Publications, including but not limited to background material, copy (drafts), original artwork, photo negatives, prints, flats, etc. This includes all work performed both inside and outside the agency.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Successive and
substantive drafts of major
publications may be archival.
Major is defined by both the
publication's authorship and its
impact on Texas and Texans,
Original artwork, including photo
prints and negatives that have
significant value as evidence of
agency programs as well as the
potential for re-use, may be
archival. See AIN 1.36 for final
State Publications created from
development files.

series_description: Relatively long-lived computer files containing organized and consistent sets of complete and accurate electronic records and associated processing files. Examples include, but are not limited to, date tables of relational databases used by applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of AC
to agency master files and
application data that the files do
not fall under records series listed
elsewhere.

series_description: Agency-developed automated software applications, operating system files, and associated processing files, including job control language, programs, applications, scripts, source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description: Records documenting operational and maintenance requirements of computer hardware such as operating manuals, hardware/operating system requirements, hardware configurations, equipment control systems, and associated processing files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Hardware needed for access to
electronic records must be retained
for the period of time required to
access the records.

series_description: Records adequate to specify all technical characteristics necessary for reading or processing electronic records; to document modification to computer programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate, and interpret data in an automated system; and allow for their timely, authorized disposition, records include but are not limited to: user guides, system or sub-system definitions, system specifications, input and output specifications, system flow charts, program flow carts, program maintenance logs, changes notes, data element dictionaries, file layouts, code books or tables, and other records that explain the meaning, purpose, structure, logical relationships, and origin of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and software
needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the
period of time required to access
the records.

series_description: Forms and logs used to enter and reconcile data sets submitted for processing.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
It is agency policy to retain this
records series longer than the
TSLAC retention period.

series_description: Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Agency copies of information relating to the selection by employees of life, disability, health, vision, dental, disability and other types of insurance or benefits offered by the State of Texas to its employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Includes notes of interviews with candidates; questions asked of applicants; audio and videotapes of job interviews; driving record and previous injury checks; preemployment physical examinations; polygraph examination results; and all other records that document the selection process, except for those noted in Remarks.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Records relating to the review of employee grievances against personnel policies, working conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the
U.S. Department of Labor (DOL).
See AIN 1.18 .

series_description: Job evaluations, performance appraisals, or other similar documents used to evaluate the performance of an employee.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description: Disciplinary actions are those actions which may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are imposed to discipline an employee whose conduct is harmful to the best interests of the state, the agency, or the employee work force; and for failure to improve performance or conduct following imposition of corrective action. May include cause for demotion, suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons for failure to give written notice of resignation. May also be used to document evidence of employee self- improvement efforts, as well as favorable and unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Forms or similar records used to create or charge information concerning the records of an employee, including pay grade, position classification, employee number, evaluation date, and termination of employment.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description: Job descriptions, including all associated task or skill statements, for positions in an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Criminal history record information on job applicants or agency employees obtained from the Department of Public Safety (DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: The agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_description: Certificates of completion, certifications, licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar records documenting the training, testing, certification, licensing, or continuing education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Form completed and signed by employee or official, or former employee or official, electing to keep home address, home telephone number, social security number, and family information open or confidential under the Public Information Act
comments:
CAUTION: Most recent public
access election information must
be kept as long as the former
employee verification records. See
AIN 3.21.

series_description: Documentation used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary or required deductions from payroll, including garnishment or other court-ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Payroll records and registers documenting employee earnings, wages, and pay. This may include but is not limited to payroll input records, summary statements, payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and payroll history.
retention_statement: 4 years

series_description: Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax records
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Employer's copy of "Employee's Withholding Exemption Certificate".
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Records and documentation relating to unemployment compensation claims, including reimbursement of funds disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission (TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Records documenting the amount of pension or deferred compensation earned by individual employees.
retention_statement: 4 years

series_description: Minimum information needed to verify employment, includes: name, social security number; exact dates of employment; last known address and most recent public access option form.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years
comments: See AIN 3.14.

series_description: Reports to the agency from TWC or its predecessor pertaining to employees.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description: Any internally distributed manuals, guidelines, or similar records that define agency wide policies and procedures concerning the personnel of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description: Includes documentation used to complete EEO (Equal Employment Opportunity) reports.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description: Records documenting individual employee's hours worked, including work schedules and documentation evidencing adherence to or deviation from normal hours for those employees working on fixed schedules.
retention_statement: 4 years

series_title: Time Off and/or Sick Leave Requests
series_description: Requests and authorizations for vacation, compensatory, sick, Family and Medical Leave Act (FLMA), sick pool leave, and other types of authorized leave, and supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Claims; invoices; statements; copies of checks and purchase orders; expenditure authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance, and special vouchers; and similar records that serve to document disbursements, including those documenting claims for and reimbursement to employees for travel and other expenses.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing registers, account cards, deposit warrants, cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts books, cash transfers, daily cash reports, reconciliations, general journal vouchers, special vouchers, and similar records that serve to document money owed to or received by a state agency and its collection or receipt.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Internal and external periodic fiscal management reports and associated worksheets, including federal financial reports, salary reports, HUB reports, investments performance reports, etc.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: This series does not
include fiscal reports created to
fulfill grant requirements.

series_description: Annual Financial Reports (AFR) required by the General Appropriations Act (100 Day Report).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Annual Financial Reports must
be submitted to the Texas State
publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(a). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.
CAUTION: If an agency does not
produce a biennial or annual
narrative report as described in AIN
1.26, then these annual financial
reports must be marked with
Archival Code A. The archival
requirement, when a biennial or
annual narrative report is not
produced, is met by sending the
required copies of the reports to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description: Periodic reports compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Yearly report compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Records of accounts deemed uncollectible, including write-off authorizations and return checks, warrants, and drafts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description: Documentation relating to the capital and fixed assets of a state agency, including equipment or property history cards containing data on initial cost, depreciation schedules or summaries used for capital outlay budgeting, and property sale, auction, or disposal records of agency owned equipment and property.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description: Signature cards or similar records establishing authority of an agency employee to initiate or authorize financial transactions on behalf of an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description: Bank statements, credit card statements, cancelled checks, check registers, deposit slips, debit and credit notices, reconciliations, and other banking related records.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Required by the General Appropriations Act. Includes encumbrances and documentation about budget transfers and revisions, as well as detail charts of account.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Contracts, leases, and agreements include general obligation, land lease, utilities, and construction except for buildings. Documents include specifications, affidavits of publication of calls for bids, performance bonds, contracts, purchase orders, inspection reports, and correspondence. May also include other applicable documentation in the master contract file per Texas Comptroller of Public Accounts Contact Management Guide. Executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
See related AIN 5.7 a/b for bid
documentation.

series_description: Any mailing address, telephone or fax number, or e-mail address records maintained by an agency on its employees or on entities or persons it serves.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description: Records and report of postage expenses, including postal meter usage.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Records documenting the inventorying, maintenance, usage checkout, and disposal of supplies, equipment, and property of a state agency. Includes lost, stolen, and damage reports.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
See AIN 5.5 for the maintenance
logs of individual pieces of
equipment.

series_title: Inspection, Repair, and Maintenance Records- Equipment
series_description: Equipment history file, which may include logbooks and requests for installation, moves, service, repair, etc.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description: Reports summarizing efficiency of facility space utilization, which may include data on room usage, demand, allocation, and capacity
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description: Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, successful and unsuccessful bids, and bid tabulation/evaluations. Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or before September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Includes bid requisition/authorizations, invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications, successful and unsuccessful bids, and bid tabulation/evaluations. Associated with a contract executed, renewed, or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Invalid bids that do not meet agency submission requirements and are not included in bid evaluation process (e.g. withdrawn, missed submission deadline, incomplete submission, etc.)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Log, register, etc., providing a record of purchase orders issued, orders received, and similar data on procurement status.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Logs, registers, or similar records documenting visitors to limited access or restricted areas of agency facilities
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description: Records relating to the issuance of keys, identification cards, building passes, passwords, signed statements or similar instruments of access to agency or state facilities, equipment or automated systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Investigative materials
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Transcripts and final decisions of hearings conducted as part of the regulatory process, and hearings on proposed rules and changes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only records that
lead to substantial or statutory
changes to the operations of the
agency and its policies and
procedures should be retained for
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.
CAUTION: These records may be
maintained with related
information, including meeting
minutes, and require a longer
retention period.

series_description: Agency final order
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description: Investigation report of allegations and findings.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description: Application materials and supporting documentation submitted by the applicant, together with any documentation generated by agency staff in connection with the processing of the application.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
